concerns.text: FR-001 mandates preprocessing dMRI using MRtrix with HCP multimodal parcellation (360 parcels). The plan.md 'Technical Context' and 'Project Structure' list 'MRtrix/NetworkX wrapper' but do not explicitly commit to the 360-parcel parcellation or the specific MRtrix3 workflow required to generate the structural connectome. Without this explicit commitment in the plan, the implementation may default to a different parcellation, failing FR-001.

FR-004 requires z-score normalization of the EEG signal before thresholding. The plan.md 'Research' section (2.1) and 'Technical Context' mention 'MNE pipeline for cleaning/avalanche detection' but omit the specific requirement for z-score normalization prior to the 75th percentile threshold. This is a critical algorithmic step for FR-004 compliance.

FR-007 requires a non-parametric permutation test with 1000 shuffles. The plan.md 'Research' section (2.1) mentions '1000-shuffle permutation test', but the 'Technical Context' and 'Project Structure' do not explicitly define the permutation logic (shuffling subject labels) or the family-wise error rate correction method in the implementation plan, relying only on the research note. The plan needs a specific task or contract entry for this logic.

FR-009 mandates VIF diagnostics and a specific flagging behavior (VIF ≥ 5 -> 'high collinearity' flag). The plan.md 'Research' section (2.1) mentions VIF calculation, but the 'Project Structure' (analysis/stats.py) and 'Data Model' do not explicitly define the output schema or logic for the 'high collinearity' flag required by the spec. The plan risks implementing VIF calculation without the mandatory conditional flagging.

FR-011 requires a likelihood ratio test between power-law, exponential, and log-normal distributions, and only reporting the exponent if power-law is preferred. The plan.md 'Research' section (2.2) mentions 'Model Comparison' and 'Likelihood ratio test', but the 'Data Model' (AvalancheMetrics) and 'Contracts' (output.schema.yaml) do not explicitly include fields for the model comparison results (e.g., 'preferred_model', 'likelihood_ratio_p_value') or the logic to suppress the exponent if power-law is not preferred. The plan may output exponents regardless of model fit.

SC-004 requires measuring the proportion of participants with complete dMRI and EEG preprocessing pipelines. The plan.md 'Data Model' and 'Contracts' define 'is_valid' and 'is_matched', but do not explicitly define a metric or output field for the 'proportion of participants with complete pipelines' (quality control rate). The plan needs a specific output or report step to measure this success criterion.
